intro: The biggest real distinction between AWS Amplify and Medplum is straightforward: AWS Amplify is aimed at Mobile, Teams, whereas Medplum targets Healthcare startups building EHR or patient-facing apps on FHIR with modern tooling. AWS Amplify sits at freemium with a paid tier from $15/month, enterprise plan available and carries low lock-in, while Medplum sits at freemium with paid upgrades and carries low lock-in. AWS Amplify is a fit when you need mobile, teams — typical scenarios include mvp/prototyping speed. Medplum is aimed at healthcare startups building ehr or patient-facing apps on fhir with modern tooling, with workloads like open-source fhir-native ehr backend for digital health teams as the common case. The honest trade-off: AWS Amplify is explicitly not for complex custom business logic; Medplum is not intended for non-healthcare projects or teams that can't invest in learning fhir. Neither tool is a drop-in replacement for the other in every scenario — the choice should track your constraints on budget, lock-in, and target workload rather than general popularity.
quick_take: AWS Amplify is for mobile, teams; Medplum is for healthcare startups building ehr or patient-facing; decide based on which workload you actually run.
verdict_a: Choose AWS Amplify if your primary workload is mobile, teams and the free tier plus $15/month paid tier fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose Medplum if your primary workload is healthcare startups building ehr or patient-facing apps on fhir with modern tooling and low switching cost matters.

faq.q: Is AWS Amplify cheaper than Medplum?
faq.a: AWS Amplify publishes an entry price of $15/month; Medplum's entry price is not in our data, so a definitive comparison is not possible without a quote from Medplum.

faq.q: Can I migrate from AWS Amplify to Medplum?
faq.a: Migrating from AWS Amplify to Medplum is generally feasible because AWS Amplify has low lock-in, so data and configuration are usually portable. Since both tools sit in the same category (Backend-as-a-Service), the conceptual mapping is closer, but you still need to validate feature parity against your specific workload. Plan migration as a project, not a script: inventory integrations, test on non-production workloads, and keep AWS Amplify running in parallel until Medplum is verified.

Our data gives AWS Amplify and Medplum the same developer experience score (4/5), so neither has a clear edge on DX by that measure. The differentiator is usually how each tool fits your workflow — things like CLI quality, docs, local dev loop, and error messages. The safest call is to pilot both on a representative task and see which one gets out of your way.

faq.q: Is Medplum a good alternative to AWS Amplify?
faq.a: Medplum is in the same category as AWS Amplify (Backend-as-a-Service), so it's a reasonable alternative to evaluate. Medplum is aimed at healthcare startups building ehr or patient-facing apps on fhir with modern tooling, while AWS Amplify targets mobile, teams — that difference should drive the switch decision. Note that Medplum is explicitly not for non-healthcare projects or teams that can't invest in learning fhir — if that describes your workload, it's not a viable replacement.
